item.title: BP, Mubadala and Inpex among winners in Indonesia bid round
item.description: Southeast Asian nation has awarded nine exploration assets to a mix of international and domestic players

item.title: Israel acted alone on South Pars attack, Netanyahu confirms
item.description: The prime minister also suggested Gulf oil exports can be rerouted through Israel to avoid the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Operator confirms FPSO will be suitable for Timor Leste project
item.description: The Petrojarl I has a long history of being used on multiple fields

item.title: Oil falls from Thursday highs amid latest volatile trading day
item.description: Brent flirts with $119 before falling to $108

item.title: Qatar: LNG plants could take five years to repair after missile strikes
item.description: Attacks wiped out 17% of country’s export capacity, could lead to $20 billion in lost revenue annually

item.title: Boost or bane? Surging oil prices a mixed blessing for supermajors
item.description: Soaring crude prices set to boost supermajors' profits in short term but could threaten shift in longer term demand trends

item.title: Eni touts floating LNG for big Venezuela field
item.description: Italian major hints that floating liquefaction vessel could be deployed on Perla field after 2030

item.title: European nations pledge to help guide ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands join Japan in calling for moratorium on attacks to oil and gas facilities

item.title: Drone falls on Saudi refinery
item.description: Saudi Arabia still assessing damage, defence ministry says

item.title: Drone falls on Saudi refinery targeted by Iran
item.description: Saudi Arabia still assessing damage, defence ministry says

item.title: Gazprom claims Ukraine seeking to disrupt gas supply to Turkey and Europe
item.description: Russian gas giant alleges recent intensification in attacks despite disruption to global gas supplies caused by Iran war

item.title: US repeats threats to Kharg Island following Iranian attacks on Persian Gulf energy sites
item.description: US mission against Iran remains unchanged since the start of the war, says defence secretary

item.title: Kharg Island strike shows US 'controls' Iran's fate, says Hegseth
item.description: US mission against Iran remains unchanged since the start of the war, says defence secretary

item.title: Eni targets up to 4% production growth by 2030
item.description: Current project portfolio offers ‘best opportunity in company’s history’, says CEO Descalzi

item.title: Afentra confirms it is acquisition target
item.description: Angola-focused player in talks with multiple parties

item.title: Afentra confirms it is acquisition target - stock price up 14%
item.description: Angola-focused player in talks with multiple parties

item.title: Shell assessing damage after attack on world’s largest gas-to-liquids plant in Qatar
item.description: The Pearl GTL is a joint venture between state giant QatarEnergy and Shell and is the world’s largest gas-to-liquids plant

item.title: Sonangol joins European juniors in Angolan acquisition
item.description: State oil company joins with Afentra andf M&P to boost stakes in shallow water blocks

item.title: Energean suspends 2026 Israel guidance amid Iran war
item.description: Conflict has caused production shutdowns across the Middle East

item.title: Kuwaiti refinery attacked by drones
item.description: Iran has launched retaliatory strikes following Israeli attack on South Pars facility

item.title: European gas prices soar following Qatar LNG strikes
item.description: Europe's TTF benchmark rose 67% last week alone, hitting highest level since early 2023

item.title: BP seals deal to sell German refinery
item.description: The company has pledged to dispose of assets totalling $20 billion by the end of next year

item.title: Greek parliament ratifies Chevron exploration licences
item.description: Energy Minister says final approval 'weakens' Libya-Turkey exclusive economic zone deal

item.title: Major Adnoc oil and gas facilities shut down after missile attack
item.description: The ongoing Middle East war has impacted several facilities operated by Adnoc

item.title: Missile strikes hit multiple QatarEnergy LNG facilities, triggering ‘extensive’ damage in fresh escalation
item.description: Qatar has a nameplate LNG production capacity of 77 million tonnes per annum and is a key natural gas supplier

item.title: ‘Extensive’ damage from fresh missile strikes on QatarEnergy LNG facilities
item.description: Qatar has a nameplate LNG production capacity of 77 million tonnes per annum and is a key natural gas supplier

item.title: Green light for Southeast Asian FPSO field development plan
item.description: Jadestone has secured government approval for its NDUM field development plan

item.title: Sinopec’s engineering arm cheers record $14 billion in new contracts
item.description: Sinopec Oilfield Service Corporation reported strong overseas performance in 2025

item.title: Record Arctic Alaska auction lures ExxonMobil, Shell, Repsol, ConocoPhillips
item.description: BLM says National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska lease sale was strongest ever held for drilling rights in the region

item.title: Record Arctic Alaska auction lures ExxonMobil, Shell, Repsol and ConocoPhillips
item.description: BLM says National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska lease sale was strongest ever held for drilling rights in the region
